title: Mohar's Conjecture
statement_text: All the $k$-colorings of a graph are Kempe equivalent for $k > \Delta$.
context_text: Mohar conjectured that in the setting of Brooks' theorem, all $k$-colorings of a graph with maximum degree $\Delta$ are Kempe equivalent when $k > \Delta$. This was confirmed for non-regular graphs by Las Vergnas and Meyniel, for cubic graphs (except the 3-prism) by Feghali et al., and for $\Delta$-regular graphs with $\Delta > 4$ by Bonamy et al., but all known proofs yield potentially exponential-length sequences.
paper_contribution: Proves that all $k$-colorings of an $n$-vertex graph $G$ with maximum degree at most $\Delta \leq k$ are equivalent up to $O(n^2)$ Kempe changes (Theorem 0.1), with the sole exception being $k = 3$ and $G$ the 3-prism, thereby establishing a polynomial diameter bound for the reconfiguration graph.
notes: PDF source — stated in prose without a labeled conjecture environment.

title: Cereceda's Conjecture
statement_text: Any two $k$-colorings of a $d$-degenerate graph on $n$ vertices are equivalent up to $O(n^2)$ trivial Kempe changes (single-vertex recolorings) when $k > d + 2$.
context_text: Cereceda conjectured a quadratic bound on the number of vertex recolorings needed to transform any $k$-coloring of a $d$-degenerate graph into any other. Bousquet and Heinrich proved an $O(n^{d+3})$ upper bound, but the conjecture remains open even for $d = 2$. Bonamy and Bousquet confirmed it for graphs of bounded treewidth.
paper_contribution: Extends the treewidth confirmation of Cereceda's conjecture (due to Bonamy and Bousquet) to the setting of non-trivial Kempe changes with one fewer color: proves any two $k$-colorings of an $n$-vertex graph of treewidth $\mathrm{tw}$ are equivalent up to $O(\mathrm{tw}\cdot n^2)$ Kempe changes when $k > \mathrm{tw} + 1$ (Theorem 0.3).
notes: PDF source — stated in prose without a labeled conjecture environment. The paper's Theorem 0.3 is an analogue for non-trivial Kempe changes rather than a direct proof of Cereceda's conjecture, which remains open.

title: Polynomial Kempe sequences for degenerate graphs
statement_text: Can any two $k$-colorings of a $(k-1)$-degenerate graph on $n$ vertices always be connected by a sequence of Kempe changes whose length is polynomial in $n$?
context_text: Las Vergnas and Meyniel proved in 1981 that all $k$-colorings of a $d$-degenerate graph are Kempe equivalent when $k > d+1$, but their proof yields a sequence that may be exponential in the number of vertices. Whether a polynomial bound can be achieved is posed as an intriguing open question and is the central motivation for all three main results of the paper.
notes: PDF source — stated as an open question in the abstract and introduction without a labeled environment; no explicit attribution to prior authors.
